subject: MUS
course_description: This course explores one of a wide range of topics pertaining to the African American experience in the fields of human and civil rights, social and literary texts, and the social sciences.
course_notes: Topic Title: Imagining Democracy Lab This class is the first part of a two-course sequence associated with the Imagining Democracy Lab, a Mellon Foundation-funded initiative to link imagination, knowledge, and action to advance civic and political engagement. In this class, we will engage with academic perspectives from the humanities and social sciences on the concept of democracy, then examine how these perspectives have (or have not) manifested in the experience of communities in the Atlanta area specifically and American democracy more broadly. To this end, we will learn from leaders of nationally renowned civic and political engagement organizations in Georgia in addition to faculty from Emory and other institutions. We will explore course material through a mix of lectures, discussion sessions, group presentations, and an individual final research paper that entails deep study of themes from the course. An application is required to be filled out and approved before enrollment in AAS 385 for Fall 2026 is granted. The application is available via the link below and due April 15th: https://form.jotform.com/260564560180151 NO FRESHMAN
